content:
Hi.
I'd like to try again compiling Rust for Linux using rustc_codegen_gcc, but one issue I'm facing is that I use a more recent nightly, so the alloc crate won't compile.
For the purpose of doing tests locally, I'd like to update the alloc crate in Rust for Linux.
Is there any script to do that?
If not, how can I do that?
Thanks.
Would 1.70.0 be recent enough? If so, you could use https://github.com/ojeda/linux/tree/rust-1.70 (it is a branch that will be this cycle in mainline).

If you mean the rust branch, yes, I already merged 1.70.0 there, but it is a deprecated branch with the old stuff we had pre-merge. I would recommend using rust-next, or simply Linus' repo instead -- either may be easier to compile than rust, too, since there are less things there.
So you need a more recent thing than 1.70.0, then, right?
It's not going to work because it uses the box_free lang item that was removed recently.

Miguel Ojeda said:

So you need a more recent thing than 1.70.0, then, right?

Yes. Just for testing some stuff locally.

In that case, you can either fix alloc manually (sometimes it is easy if it is just a few things), or you can copy the new alloc on top, and then add the things we added. It is not automated because it requires checking that the things we added are actually still aligned with the other changes. But for quick testing, it is easy to just copy-paste without re-checking.

I can prepare a branch for you if you prefer (i.e. not suitable for upstreaming, but for your local testing only).
Ok, can you send me the changes you made to alloc, please?
To get a diff, you can use the instructions at the bottom of this commit message https://github.com/ojeda/linux/commit/e458d4595b120c83a1179ccd0c900941a2bc1db3
If you prefer I can send you the generated diff for 1.70.0
Sent it to your email -- hope that helps. And thanks for taking a look at compiling the kernel again!

I'm now getting this error:

error: symbol `__rust_alloc` is already defined
  --> rust/kernel/allocator.rs:36:1
   |
36 | fn __rust_alloc(size: usize, _align: usize) -> *mut u8 {
   | ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

Any idea how to fix it?
Those are unneeded now since 1.71.0, see https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/issues/68 and @bjorn3's patch there.
